text.title: Research Frameworks Router Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules grounding the Research Frameworks Router methodology. Every rule pairs a binary check with sourced rationale, plus the universal skip-this-methodology gate.

rule.statement: Emit exactly one picked framework per decision-record; never list 'use all three'.
rule.rationale: Mashing 3 frameworks dilutes output shape and burns tokens.

rule.statement: Picked framework MUST name the methodology slug to load next (e.g., 'market-research-tam-sam-som').
rule.rationale: Without a concrete slug, the next agent has to guess which methodology file to open.

rule.statement: The pick MUST factor product stage (ideation / pre-PMF / post-PMF / scale).
rule.rationale: TAM/SAM/SOM at scale wastes effort; Lean Canvas at scale is too coarse.

rule.statement: Decision-record MUST log >=2 rejected candidates with one-line reasons.
rule.rationale: An unconsidered alternative is a hidden assumption.

rule.statement: Apply this methodology only if all entries in `## Applies If` hold and none of `## Skip If` triggers; otherwise abort and route to the correct methodology.

rationale.source: F-066 spec — decision tree minimum form

rule.rationale: A misrouted methodology produces wrong-shape output; the explicit skip rule is the universal default branch from the decision tree.
